module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

learningObjectives.title: Handle P1 incidents under hypercare
learningObjectives.description: Execute rapid triage and restoration steps during critical go-live disruptions.

learningObjectives.title: Diagnose integration-driven planning failures
learningObjectives.description: Identify why transportation demand and Freight Unit generation stopped.

learningObjectives.title: Coordinate technical and business response
learningObjectives.description: Work with Basis, Security and business stakeholders with frequent updates.

learningObjectives.title: Drive post-incident stabilization
learningObjectives.description: Define monitoring and preventive controls after service restoration.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 9: Hypercare - Priority 1 (P1) Production Issue

sections.content: The SAP TM project has gone live successfully. It is now Day 3 of the Hypercare period. At 8:15 AM, the Transportation Control Tower receives multiple calls stating that no Freight Orders are being created for today's customer deliveries. More than 250 outbound deliveries are waiting for transportation planning.

sections.title: Incident Severity
sections.content: Priority: P1 (Critical). Business operations across India are stopped. Every minute of downtime increases delivery delays and financial losses.

You are the SAP TM Hypercare Consultant. The Incident Manager has assigned the ticket to you. Your responsibility is to restore transportation planning as quickly as possible while keeping business stakeholders informed.

After reviewing the logs, you discover that a scheduled integration job responsible for transferring transportation-relevant delivery data from SAP S/4HANA to SAP TM failed overnight because of an authorization change introduced during a security update. Since no deliveries reached SAP TM, Freight Units could not be generated.

sections.title: Resolution Analysis
rows: Temporary recovery if authorization is corrected.
Ignore issue until next job cycle

sections.content: Coordinate with the Basis and Security teams to restore the missing authorization. Restart the failed integration job, verify that transportation demand is replicated successfully, confirm Freight Unit generation, execute transportation planning and validate Freight Order creation with the business team before closing the P1 incident.

sections.title: Hypercare Best Practice
sections.content: During a P1 incident, restoring business operations is the first priority. Permanent root cause analysis and preventive actions should be completed after production stability has been restored.

sections.title: Real Production Incident
sections.content: A manufacturing company experienced a four-hour logistics outage after an overnight security role update blocked integration jobs. Although SAP TM itself was healthy, transportation planning stopped because no delivery data entered the system. Restoring the authorization resolved the issue immediately.

sections.title: Accenture P1 Scenario
sections.content: If Freight Orders suddenly stop being created after Go-Live, never assume the planning engine is at fault. First verify whether transportation demand, master data and system integrations are reaching SAP TM before investigating planning configuration.

items: Project continues to Lab 10: Final End-to-End Consultant Challenge.
